rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON VS. ALBERT H WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT ALBERT WONG, JOYCE WONG DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. As to all causes of action, there is no statute of limitations problem-the FAC states that the error was discovered in 2017 and the case was filed the same year. As to the second and third causes of action for reformation and declaratory relief, Plaintiff has standing. The FAC alleges that Plaintiff is the current, lawful owner and holder of the Note and beneficial interest under the Deed of Trust.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL OKA ET AL VS. BRICK AND TIMBER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT 204-11 LP HOLDINGS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement is DENIED, without prejudice. The Court was provided with insufficient facts necessary to evaluate the settlement. (City of Grand Terrance v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1263.) The Court was unable to make a rough approximation of Plaintiff's recovery or Defendant 204's liability. Defendant 204 did not provide the Court with sufficient evidence that Plaintiffs' alleged harm did not occur during their ownership of the subject premises, or that they have zero liability for Plaintiffs' alleged harm after Defendant 204's ownership of the subject premises expired.=(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MARK BECHELLI ET AL VS. JOHN C. KILROY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT JOHN KILROY, KILROY REALTY CORPORATION, KR FLOWER MART, LLC, KR CFM, INC., KILROY REALTY, L.P. Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel is GRANTED. Plaintiffs' counsel violated the State Fund rule by examining the Privileged Documents more than necessary to ascertain their confidential nature. Once counsel was notified, and an examination showed the documents had been transmitted between an attorney and an officer or employee of that party, that examination would suffice to ascertain the materials are privileged, and any further examination exceeded permissible limits. (State Comp. Ins. Fund v. WPS, Inc. (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 644, 656-57; see also Rico v. Mitsubishi Motors Corp. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 807, 818 [courts must consider how much review was reasonably necessary to draw that conclusion, and when counsel's examination should have ended.] Contrary to Plaintiff's counsel's assertions, counsel was not permitted to decide on its own whether the privilege applied by examining the documents. (See, Dermott Will & Emery LLP v. Superior Court (2017) 10 Cal.App.5th 1083, 1112.) Plaintiffs' counsel also violated CCP 2031.285(d)(2), which prohibits Plaintiffs' counsel from "using the information in any manner," and section 2031.285(c)(1), which "preclude[s a party] from using or disclosing the specified information until the claim of privilege is resolved" when it used the contents of the Privileged Documents in their Clawback Motion. There is no carve-out in either provision, even in regard to a motion challenging the asserted privilege. Finally, "disqualification [] serves the useful purpose of eliminating from the case the attorney who could most effectively exploit the unfair advantage [acquired through the earlier review and use of the inadvertently disclosed, privileged materials]." (Gregori v. Bank of America (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 291, 309.) Plaintiff's counsel downplays the relevance of Exhibit 2.16, however, based on his January 12, 2023 email, he discusses a privileged document that he believes he could use to prove his claims of fraud. Based on this email, the information would likely be exploited against Defendants during trial on their fraud claim. Although trial is May 15, 2023, the Court is compelled to disqualify Plaintiffs' counsel, John O'Connor, and his law firm, O'Connor and Associates.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
